source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a scaled multi-story structural frame model for a student architectural structural-design benchmark, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a multi-story frame model of this class is by construction a built-up balsa/basswood structure composed of vertical columns, horizontal beams (including the roof beam), diagonal bracing, floor/roof decks, and PVA-bonded joints. Self-weight, roof-deflection under vertical load, inter-story drift under lateral load, and buckling of slender bracing all reference the assembled frame geometry.

The model must span 600 mm between two base supports and stand 800 mm tall (representative of a scaled multi-story frame). Construction material is limited to balsa wood, basswood, and paper-based laminates with PVA adhesive; metal fasteners and composite tows are prohibited per the competition rulebook.

Assembly layout. (A) Column: vertical members running from base to roof at each corner of the frame (>= 4). (B) Beam: horizontal members tying columns at each story (including the roof-level beam carrying the 30 kg load) and at intermediate stories (>= 4 total across stories). (C) Diagonal bracing: slender struts/ties providing lateral stiffness and conditioning the first buckling mode (>= 2). (D) Floor/roof deck: paper laminate horizontal plate distributing vertical point load into the roof beam and conditioning inter-story drift at the load point.

The self-weight of the structural model must not exceed 1.0 kg. Under a vertical static load of 30 kg applied centrally at the roof level, the maximum downward deflection at the roof must not exceed L/400 where L = 800 mm (the height is treated as L for roof drift scaling in the rulebook), giving 2.0 mm. Under a horizontal static load of 5 kg applied at the roof level (representing a lateral seismic proxy), the inter-story drift must not exceed H/200 where H is the full height 800 mm, giving 4.0 mm. The model must carry both the vertical and horizontal loads simultaneously without plastic failure at any joint.

Assembly policy. Self-weight (R1) sums columns + beams + bracing + deck masses. Roof vertical deflection (R2) is a composite stiffness of columns + roof beam + deck. Inter-story drift (R3) is dominated by diagonal-bracing stiffness and beam-column joint behavior. First buckling load factor (R4) occurs in slender bracing/column members whose critical load is conditioned by beam + deck restraint - an assembly-scope property. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static structural FEA with balsa treated as orthotropic linear elastic (along-grain E approximately 3.4 GPa). Because slender bracing members dominate the lateral system, a linear buckling eigenvalue check under combined gravity + lateral load must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 1.2. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the full structural model, a bill of materials, and a report listing self-weight in grams, roof vertical deflection in mm, inter-story drift under lateral load in mm, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a scaled multi-story structural frame model for a student architectural structural-design benchmark, delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: >= 4 corner columns + >= 4 horizontal beams (including roof beam) + >= 2 slender diagonal braces + paper-laminate floor/roof decks + PVA adhesive joints. Span 600 mm between two base supports, height 800 mm. Allowed materials: balsa, basswood, paper laminates, PVA adhesive. Prohibited: metal fasteners, composite tows.

Load cases (applied simultaneously without plastic failure at any joint).
- LC1 vertical: 30 kg central at the roof.
- LC2 horizontal: 5 kg at the roof (lateral seismic proxy).

Pass limits: self-weight <= 1.0 kg; roof downward deflection under LC1 <= L/400 = 2.0 mm (L = 800 mm); inter-story drift under LC2 <= H/200 = 4.0 mm (H = 800 mm); first buckling load factor under combined gravity + lateral >= 1.2.

prompt.title: Scaled multi-story student structural frame as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 600 x 800 mm balsa/basswood multi-story frame: columns + beams + diagonal bracing + floor/roof deck; carries 30 kg vertical + 5 kg horizontal; self-weight <= 1.0 kg.

parts.name: column
parts.role: vertical member running from base to roof

parts.name: beam
parts.role: horizontal member tying columns at each story (including roof)

parts.name: diagonal_bracing
parts.role: slender strut/tie providing lateral stiffness; dominates buckling mode

parts.name: floor_roof_deck
parts.role: paper laminate deck distributing vertical point load at roof

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: beam-column moment/shear transfer; conditions drift and roof deflection
lateral load reacted via bracing; dominates first buckling mode (R4)
30 kg roof point load distributed from deck into roof beam (R2 driver)

fem_check_policy.rationale: Roof vertical deflection (R2) is a composite stiffness of columns + roof beam + deck. Inter-story drift (R3) depends on diagonal-bracing stiffness and beam-column joint behavior. First buckling load factor (R4) is conditioned by beam + deck restraint of slender bracing/columns. Self-weight (R1) sums all part families.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A scaled multi-story structural model is intrinsically an assembly of columns, beams, bracing, and deck with PVA-bonded joints; the rulebook pass/fail quantities are assembly-level.

eval_coverage: combined gravity + lateral loads on assembly
drift ratio H/200 through bracing + joints
roof deflection L/400 through deck + roof beam + columns
buckling of slender bracing with beam/deck restraints

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
